structured.intro: Persian Gulf transit volumes have collapsed to **6 vessels/day from a baseline of 110/day** (−95%) following coordinated Iranian attacks on **HMM NAMU, BARAKAH, MSC FRANCESCA, EPAMINONDAS, MAYUREE NAREE, and EUPHORIA between April 22–May 4**, with major carriers including **Maersk, MSC, Hapag-Lloyd, and CMA CGM** suspending services and war risk premiums becoming unquotable on tanker tonnage. AG–East VLCC freight rates have spiked to **$172k/day versus $28k baseline** amid a 6x rate premium, dark vessel detections at 16 in 24 hours, and widespread vessel-operator diversions to bypass pipelines, signaling a structural market disruption that now threatens regional hydrocarbon exports and global crude supply chains.
